title: Allium yesilyurtense

The genus Allium is a group of plants commonly known as onions or garlic, with over 700 species. The species Allium yesilyurtense is a type of wild garlic or onion native to Turkey. It is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the subgenus Melanocrommyum. Its classification is further supported by its unique characteristics and genetic makeup.
